recipes.name: Zesty Ginger-Garlic Chicken Stir-Fry
ingredients: 450g chicken breast (or thighs), cut into bite-sized pieces
steps: Cook rice according to package instructions. Keep warm.
Marinate chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp minced ginger, 1 clove minced garlic, 1/2 tbsp cornstarch, salt, and pepper. Set aside for 10 minutes.

Prepare stir-fry sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together remaining 3 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp minced ginger, 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, remaining 1/2 tbsp cornstarch, and 2 tbsp water or broth.

Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and stir-fry until cooked through and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp vegetable oil to the same pan. Add broccoli and red bell pepper, stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return cooked chicken to the pan. Pour the prepared stir-fry sauce over the chicken and vegetables. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats everything, about 1-2 minutes.
Stir in sesame oil. Serve immediately over the hot cooked rice, garnished with sliced green onions if desired.

recipes.name: Thai-Inspired Coconut Chili Chicken Rice Bowl
ingredients: 450g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 tbsp red curry paste (adjust to spice preference)
1 cup chicken broth or water (for rice)
1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish
1 red chili, sliced (optional, for extra heat)
steps: Rinse rice thoroughly. In a medium saucepan, combine rice with 1 cup chicken broth/water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until water is absorbed and rice is tender. Keep warm.
While rice cooks, marinate chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, grated ginger, and 1 minced garlic clove. Let sit for 10 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add remaining 2 minced garlic cloves and red curry paste; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add marinated chicken to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.

Pour in coconut milk, remaining 2 tbsp soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and sauce has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed (add more soy sauce for saltiness, lime for tartness, brown sugar for sweetness).
Serve the coconut chili chicken over the hot cooked rice. Garnish with fresh cilantro and sliced red chili if desired.

recipes.name: Smoky Chipotle-Ginger Chicken & Rice Skillet
ingredients: 450g chicken breast (or thighs), cut into 1-inch pieces
1.5 tbsp fresh ginger, grated or finely minced (divided)
1 tsp minced chipotle chili in adobo sauce (or more to taste)
1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish
steps: Marinate chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 0.5 tbsp ginger, 1 minced garlic clove, 0.5 tsp cumin, salt, and pepper. Set aside for at least 10 minutes.

Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large, oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.

Add remaining 1 tbsp vegetable oil to the skillet. Add diced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add bell pepper, remaining 1 tbsp ginger, 3 minced garlic cloves, remaining 0.5 tsp cumin, and oregano. Cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring to coat the grains. Add diced tomatoes (undrained), chicken broth, remaining 2 tbsp soy sauce, and minced chipotle chili. Bring to a boil.

Return chicken to the skillet. Stir everything together, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed. If the rice is still too firm, add a little more broth and continue cooking.
Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro and lime wedges.

recipes.name: Fragrant Indian-Spiced Chicken Pilaf
ingredients: 500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1.5-inch pieces
3 tbsp fresh ginger, grated or made into a paste (divided)
4 cloves garlic, minced or made into a paste (divided)
0.25 cup fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish
steps: Wash basmati rice thoroughly and soak in cold water for 20 minutes, then drain well. This helps with fluffy rice.

Marinate chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1.5 tbsp ginger paste, 2 cloves garlic paste, yogurt, garam masala, turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder. Mix well and let marinate for at least 20 minutes (or longer in the fridge).
Heat vegetable oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add sliced onion and cook until golden brown and softened, about 8-10 minutes.
Add remaining 1.5 tbsp ginger paste and 2 cloves garlic paste to the pot. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add marinated chicken to the pot and cook, stirring, until the chicken is browned on all sides, about 7-8 minutes.
Stir in diced tomato and cook for 3-4 minutes until it starts to break down. Pour in chicken broth (or water) and remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce. Bring to a simmer.

Add the drained rice and salt to taste. Stir gently to combine. Bring back to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until rice is cooked and liquid is absorbed.
Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5-10 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork. Stir in chopped fresh mint if using.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.

recipes.name: Mediterranean Lemon-Herb Chicken with Ginger-Garlic Pilaf
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated or finely minced (divided)
4 cloves garlic, minced (2 for chicken, 2 for rice)
1.5 cups uncooked long-grain white rice (or Arborio)
3 tbsp olive oil (2 for chicken, 1 for rice)
0.25 cup fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish
steps: Marinate chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken thighs with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp lemon zest, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.5 tbsp grated ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic, oregano, thyme, salt, and pepper. Mix well and let marinate for at least 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add remaining 0.5 tbsp grated ginger and 2 cloves minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add rice to the skillet and toast for 2-3 minutes, stirring, until grains are lightly translucent.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a simmer. Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange marinated chicken thighs on top of the simmering rice in the skillet. Cover the skillet tightly with a lid or aluminum foil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and rice has absorbed all the liquid.
Remove from oven and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff the rice gently with a fork.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped parsley and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice if desired.

recipes.name: Golden Dragon Ginger-Garlic Chicken Fried Rice
ingredients: 3 cups cooked white rice (cold, day-old recommended)
2 green onions, sliced (white and green parts separated)
steps: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp vegetable oil to the wok. Add minced ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push ginger and garlic to one side, pour whisked eggs into the other side. Scramble the eggs, then break them into small pieces and mix with the aromatics.
Add diced carrots and the white parts of green onions to the wok, stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender. Add frozen peas and cook for another minute.
Add cold rice to the wok, breaking up any clumps. Pour soy sauce over the rice and stir-fry vigorously for 3-5 minutes, ensuring the rice is well coated and heated through.
Return cooked chicken to the wok. Add the green parts of the green onions. Stir everything together.
Drizzle with sesame oil, season with salt and pepper to taste, and give one final stir. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Siam Sunset Ginger-Garlic Chicken & Rice Bowl
ingredients: 4 boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 tbsp soy sauce (for marinade and sauce)
1.5 tbsp fresh ginger, grated or finely minced
2 tbsp red curry paste (adjust to spice preference)
Optional: Sliced fresh chili for garnish
steps: In a bowl, marinate chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp grated ginger, and 2 minced garlic cloves for at least 15 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining grated ginger and minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Add red curry paste and stir-fry for another minute until aromatic.
Pour in coconut milk, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a simmer, stirring well to combine the curry paste.
Return chicken to the skillet. Add sliced red bell pepper and onion. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in lime juice and the remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed (add more fish sauce for savory, sugar for sweet, lime for sour).
Serve the spicy coconut chicken and vegetables over cooked jasmine rice, garnished with fresh cilantro and optional fresh chili.

recipes.name: Umami Chicken & Saffron Risotto with Ginger Kiss
ingredients: 2 tbsp soy sauce (for glaze and risotto)
1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated (for glaze) + 1 tsp minced (for risotto)
3 cloves garlic, 2 minced for risotto, 1 minced for glaze
1/2 cup dry white wine (e.g., Pinot Grigio)
steps: Prepare the chicken glaze: whisk together 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp grated ginger, and 1 minced garlic clove. Marinate chicken breasts in this mixture for 10-15 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat. Sear chicken breasts for 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through and nicely browned. Remove and set aside, keeping warm.

In a separate heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, melt 1 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add 2 minced garlic cloves and 1 tsp minced ginger, cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add Arborio rice and stir for 1-2 minutes until the edges of the grains become translucent. Pour in white wine and stir until it's completely absorbed.

Begin adding hot chicken broth, one ladleful at a time, stirring constantly until each addition is absorbed before adding the next. This process will take about 20-25 minutes. If using, dissolve saffron in a bit of hot broth and add it midway.
Once the rice is creamy and al dente (still a slight bite), remove from heat. Stir in remaining 1 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and grated Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Slice the seared ginger-soy chicken and serve immediately over the risotto.

recipes.name: Zesty Ginger-Garlic Chicken Fajita Bowls
ingredients: Optional toppings: black beans, corn, avocado, salsa, sour cream
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken strips with 1 tbsp soy sauce, grated ginger, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp lime juice, chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the same skillet. Add sliced onion and bell peppers. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. Add remaining 2 minced garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute more.
Return cooked chicken to the skillet with the vegetables. Add remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tbsp lime juice. Stir well to combine and heat through.
Assemble the bowls: place cooked brown rice at the base. Top with the ginger-garlic chicken and fajita vegetables.
Garnish generously with fresh cilantro. Add any desired optional toppings like black beans, corn, avocado, or salsa. Serve warm.

recipes.name: Mogul's Ginger-Garlic Chicken Pilaf
ingredients: 4 boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
1.5 cups Basmati rice, rinsed and soaked for 20 minutes
1.5 tbsp fresh ginger, grated or paste
steps: In a bowl, marinate chicken pieces with 1 tbsp grated ginger, 2 minced garlic cloves, Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp soy sauce, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt, and pepper. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat ghee or oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon stick, bay leaf) and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add thinly sliced onion and cook until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add remaining 0.5 tbsp grated ginger and 2 minced garlic cloves, cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomato and cook until softened and the oil separates, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the marinated chicken to the pot and cook, stirring, until the chicken pieces are lightly seared on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Drain the soaked Basmati rice and add it to the pot. Stir gently for 1-2 minutes to coat the rice with the spices and chicken.

Pour in chicken broth (or water) and add garam masala and salt to taste. Bring to a rolling boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 15-18 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Remove from heat and let it rest, covered, for 5-10 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve.

recipes.name: Ginger-Garlic Soy Chicken Stir-fry with Steamed Jasmine Rice
ingredients: 2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 340g), cut into 1-inch pieces
1 teaspoon cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons water (for sauce)
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon grated ginger, and 2 minced garlic cloves. Marinate for at least 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, rinse jasmine rice thoroughly. Combine rice and 2 cups water in a pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add bell pepper, broccoli, and carrot to the same skillet. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons soy sauce, optional brown sugar, sesame oil, and the cornstarch slurry.
Return cooked chicken to the skillet with the vegetables. Pour the sauce mixture over the chicken and vegetables. Stir well and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Season with salt and pepper if needed. Serve immediately over the steamed jasmine rice.

recipes.name: Spicy Thai Green Curry Chicken with Fragrant Coconut Rice
ingredients: 450g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces
1 cup green beans, trimmed and halved
steps: In a bowl, toss chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon soy sauce. Set aside.

Combine jasmine rice, 1 cup water, and 1/2 cup coconut milk in a pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add green curry paste, grated ginger, and minced garlic. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chicken pieces and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the remaining full-fat coconut milk and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer, then add fish sauce. Stir well.
Add bell pepper, green beans, and bamboo shoots (if using). Continue to simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh basil leaves. Serve the green curry hot with the fragrant coconut rice and lime wedges.

recipes.name: Zesty Ginger-Soy Chicken & Garlic Rice Fiesta Bowl
ingredients: 2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 340g), cut into 1/2-inch cubes
3 cloves garlic, minced (2 for chicken, 1 for rice)
1/2 cup canned black beans, rinsed and drained
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken cubes with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, grated ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tablespoon lime juice, cumin, and chili powder. Marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Rinse rice thoroughly. In a medium pot, heat 1/2 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 minced garlic clove and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add rice and stir for 1 minute.
Pour in 2 cups water, a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.

While rice cooks, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and lightly browned, about 6-8 minutes. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add diced red onion and red bell pepper. Sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened. Add black beans and corn, cooking for another 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the chopped cilantro. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Assemble the bowls: spoon the garlic-cilantro rice into bowls, top with the cooked ginger-soy chicken, and the sautéed vegetable-bean mixture. Garnish with optional avocado slices.

recipes.name: Aromatic Ginger-Garlic Chicken Pilaf (Biryani Style)
ingredients: 450g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste (or 1 tbsp grated ginger + 1 tbsp minced garlic)
1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (or paprika for less heat)
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with soy sauce, yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, turmeric, chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Marinate for at least 20 minutes.
Rinse basmati rice thoroughly under cold water until water runs clear. Drain and set aside.

Heat ghee or oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes. Remove a few tablespoons of crispy onion for garnish and set aside.
Add cinnamon stick, cardamom pods, and bay leaves to the pot. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the marinated chicken to the pot and cook, stirring, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the chopped tomato and cook for 3-4 minutes until it softens and breaks down.
Add the rinsed basmati rice to the pot, stir gently to combine with the chicken and spices, and cook for 1-2 minutes.

Pour in the chicken broth or water and add the remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed and rice is cooked. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork, garnish with fresh cilantro and reserved crispy onions, then serve.

recipes.name: Creamy Ginger-Garlic Chicken Risotto with Parmesan
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine (e.g., Pinot Grigio or Chardonnay)
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: In a small bowl, toss diced chicken with soy sauce. Season with a pinch of black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet or pot over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
In the same pot, reduce heat to medium and add butter. Once melted, add chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 3-5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot. Sauté for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add Arborio rice to the pot and stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until the edges of the grains become translucent.

Pour in the white wine and stir until it's completely absorbed by the rice. Begin adding the hot chicken broth, one ladleful at a time, stirring constantly and waiting for each addition to be almost fully absorbed before adding the next. This process will take about 20-25 minutes.
When the rice is creamy and al dente (still has a slight bite), stir in the cooked chicken, Parmesan cheese, and chopped parsley. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan and parsley if desired.
